# RSA Asymmetric Encryption Module

Implement an RSA asymmetric encryption module that encrypts data with a public key and decrypts it with the corresponding private key.

## Capabilities

### Encrypt with public key and decrypt with private key

Use RSA public-key encryption to encrypt a short message, and use the corresponding private key to decrypt it back.

- Encrypting `"secret"` with an RSA public key and decrypting with the private key returns `"secret"` (round-trip) [@test](./tests/rsa-round-trip.test.js)
- The encrypted output is a Buffer of the RSA key modulus size (e.g. 256 bytes for 2048-bit RSA) [@test](./tests/rsa-encrypted-size.test.js)
- Encrypting the same plaintext twice produces different ciphertexts (due to OAEP random padding) [@test](./tests/rsa-random-padding.test.js)
- Decrypting with the wrong private key throws an error [@test](./tests/rsa-wrong-key.test.js)

## Implementation

[@generates](./src/index.js)

## API

```javascript { #api }
/**
 * Encrypt data using an RSA public key (OAEP padding).
 * @param {string|Buffer} plaintext - The data to encrypt
 * @param {string} publicKeyPem - PEM-encoded RSA public key
 * @returns {Buffer} Encrypted ciphertext
 */
export function rsaEncrypt(plaintext, publicKeyPem);

/**
 * Decrypt RSA-encrypted data using a private key (OAEP padding).
 * @param {Buffer} ciphertext - The encrypted data
 * @param {string} privateKeyPem - PEM-encoded RSA private key
 * @returns {Buffer} Decrypted plaintext
 */
export function rsaDecrypt(ciphertext, privateKeyPem);
```

## Dependencies { .dependencies }

### crypto-browserify { .dependency }

Provides browser-compatible implementation of Node.js's `crypto` module, including publicEncrypt and privateDecrypt for RSA asymmetric encryption.

[@satisfied-by](crypto-browserify)
